title: 10 best herbal creams for hemorrhoids

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids by suppressing prostaglandins and leukotrienes.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel content provides a protective barrier that soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing of hemorrhoid tissues.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Aloe vera's antimicrobial compounds, such as salicylic acid and lupeol, help prevent infection and reduce the risk of complications by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate hemorrhoids.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as antioxidants that promote healing and reduce swelling. It forms a protective barrier on the skin, providing instant relief from itching, burning, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Regular application of aloe vera cream can also aid in reducing inflammation and promoting the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloein, specifically aloe-emodin, has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and heal irritated skin and reduce the risk of infection.
Vitamins (A, C, E): These vitamins have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are used due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin.
Gel from Leaves: The gel obtained from the leaves is used due to its cooling and soothing properties, which help to reduce pain and itching associated with hemorrhoids.
Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract from leaves is used due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which promote healing and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in the melted beeswax.
Add 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and stir well to ensure a smooth consistency.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping with an electric mixer.
Transfer the mixture to a storage container and refrigerate for about 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Astringent Properties: The tannins present in witch hazel help to constrict and shrink swollen tissues, providing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Witch hazel's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Antiseptic Properties: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of witch hazel help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for wound healing, reducing the risk of complications and promoting recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American Witch Hazel, creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as tannins and flavonoids, constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing relief from itching, discomfort, and pain associated with hemorrhoids. The cream's soothing and cooling effects also promote healing and reduce inflammation, helping to shrink hemorrhoids and prevent further irritation, making it a popular and effective natural remedy for hemorrhoid relief.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: This tannin acts as a vasoconstrictor, reducing blood flow to the affected area and thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Oligostilbenoids (especially Vitisin A): These phenolic compounds exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, soothe irritated tissue, and promote healing in the affected area.

Sambunigine: Although this constituent is present in smaller amounts, it has been reported to possess mild anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to the overall soothing effect of Hamamelis virginiana creams on hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect irritated skin.
Barks: They are utilized for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids.

Leaves: They are used because of their high concentration of flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent effects that aid in healing and reducing discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steep the plant material in 2 cups of distilled water for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine the 1/2 cup of infused liquid with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow cream contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and shields irritated tissue, providing relief from hemorrhoidal discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal marshmallow creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin or flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.

Softering and protective effects: The mucilage in marshmallow cream helps to soften and protect the skin around the anus, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by hemorrhoidal itching, burning, and bleeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root of the plant is rich in mucilages, which provides a protective barrier on the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. The cream helps to calm the affected area, reducing pain, itching, and swelling associated with hemorrhoids. remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help soothe and protect the affected area by forming a protective barrier, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Glucosides: Glucosides, specifically the glucosides of inulin, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain caused by hemorrhoids.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Althaea officinalis, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain mucilages, which help soothe and protect irritated skin.
Leaves: They also contain mucilages, contributing to their soothing and protective properties.
Barks: The barks of Althaea officinalis contain mucilages, which aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh root material of althaea officinalis in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested root material in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried root material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered root material with 100g of distilled water in a saucepan and heat gently for 10 minutes.
Combine the cooled plant extract with 50g of petroleum jelly and 25g of beeswax in a double boiler and melt for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams help protect the skin around the anus from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications such as infection or scarring.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's saponins have been shown to enhance wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and reducing scar formation, which can help repair damaged tissue around hemorrhoids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers from a trusted source in bulk quantities for future use.
Steep 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil from the dried flowers using cheesecloth and squeeze the cheesecloth to extract remaining oil.
Mix 1 cup of the infused calendula oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whisk and heat the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter melt and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as isobutylamides and triterpenoid saponins, reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower creams, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the anal area, promoting healing and reducing pain.

Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain, inflammation, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer and early fall when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested roots and flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers with 1 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour while stirring occasionally to melt the beeswax and coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove any impurities and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoid compounds in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable supplier or wild source ensuring quality control.
Combine the dried leaves with 100 grams of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily to enhance extraction efficiency.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of vitamin e oil in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and prevent infection in hemorrhoid-prone areas.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 ounces of dried hydrastis canadensis roots.
Mix 1 ounce of dried roots with 6 ounces of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 120 to 140 degrees fahrenheit for 2 to 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Whip 4 ounces of shea butter with 2 ounces of the infused oil until smooth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any impurities.
Grind 2-3 grams of the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 20 grams of vegetable oil in a double boiler and heat it gently.
Combine 10 grams of the oil mixture with 20 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth cream texture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh ulmus rubra leaves and 1/4 cup of its flowers in the morning when dew is present.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 days.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried leaves and flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Blend the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes then pour into jars and let cool.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a trusted supplier.
Infuse 2 cups of boiling water with 1/4 cup of dried cinchona officinalis bark for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Combine the infused liquid with the beeswax mixture and whip until smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried plantago major leaves from reputable sources to ensure quality and potency.
Mix 50g of dried plantago major leaves with 500ml of warm water in a heatproof bowl.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to extract the medicinal properties into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding solids and transfer to an ice bath.
Once cooled, add 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil to the liquid and stir until fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale, drying them in shade at room temperature for 2 days.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking approximately 20 minutes.
Combine one part powdered symphytum officinale with two parts petroleum jelly in a double boiler, stirring constantly for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature that is safe to apply topically, about 1 hour.
Transfer the cooled mixture into sterile jars, labeling them with date and contents, and storing them in refrigerator at 40°f or below for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 2 hours or air drying for several days.
Combine 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and infuse in a glass jar at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl discarding solids and mixing with 10ml of beeswax and 5ml of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into sterilized cream jars and allow to cool at room temperature before capping and labeling for storage.

Combine the harvested botanicals with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil infuse for 2 hours.

Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil melt beeswax in double boiler.
Pour the warm mixture into glass jars let cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb guggul, also known as Commiphora mukul, contains compounds that reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids. When applied topically in a cream form, it soothes the affected area, reducing discomfort and irritation. Additionally, guggul's antiseptic properties help prevent infection, promoting healing and alleviating symptoms of hemorrhoids such as itching, bleeding, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Guggul's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Strengthening property: Guggul's strengthening property improves blood vessel walls' elasticity, which can help prevent further straining and irritation of the rectal area, reducing the risk of recurring hemorrhoids.

Antioxidant property: Guggul's antioxidant property helps protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hemorrhoidal symptoms such as itching, burning, and bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in a bioactive compound called guggulsterone, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in soothing hemorrhoid discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help to reduce inflammation, swelling, and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried commiphora mukul resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground commiphora mukul with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass bowl.
Add 5% by weight of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated to create a smooth consistency.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate melting of the beeswax.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then whip the cream using an electric mixer until light and airy, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with hemorrhoids because it provides natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's active ingredient, tea tree oil, has been shown to reduce swelling, ease pain, and prevent infection in the rectal region. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help eliminate bacteria that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms. As a result, using herbal tea tree creams can provide quick relief from discomfort, itching, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms, such as itching, discomfort, and pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tea tree creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the rectal area, providing relief from painful and uncomfortable hemorrhoids.
Wound-healing properties: Tea tree oil's wound-healing properties can aid in the healing of damaged tissue around hemorrhoids, reducing the risk of complications such as bleeding, pain, and itching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Abundant in oil-rich glands, tea tree leaves provide a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and protect the skin around hemorrhoids.
Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, tea tree roots offer antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that can aid in reducing inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass containers and utensils by autoclaving for 15 minutes at 121°c.
Combine 200g of petroleum jelly with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 20ml of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Continue heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 70°c, then remove from heat and let cool to 40°c.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized containers and label with date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The curcumin in turmeric reduces swelling, pain, and itching associated with hemorrhoids, providing instant relief. Additionally, turmeric's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to prevent infection and promote healing by reducing inflammation and soothing the affected area. By applying a turmeric cream topically, individuals can experience significant alleviation from hemorrhoid discomfort and irritation, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, helps reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which cause inflammation.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hemorrhoid development and exacerbate symptoms like itching and bleeding.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial property: Turmeric's bioactive compounds, including curcumin and volatile oils, have been shown to accelerate wound healing and prevent infection in the anal area, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster recovery from hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is a good source of curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including hemorrhoids, due to their ability to soothe inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried turmeric powder into a fine consistency using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 2 teaspoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine the ground turmeric powder with 5 milliliters of coconut oil and stir until well blended.
Gradually add the turmeric-coconut oil mixture to the oil-beeswax mixture while stirring constantly.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with hemorrhoids because they provide soothing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort caused by inflamed veins. The anti-inflammatory properties of sage help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, sage's antiseptic and astringent properties help prevent infection and shrink the affected area, promoting faster healing. By reducing inflammation and providing a cooling sensation, sage creams offer effective relief for individuals suffering from this uncomfortable condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms such as pain, itching, and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help soothe and calm the affected area, reducing discomfort, swelling, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids.

Wound-healing properties: Sage's ability to promote wound healing through its flavonoids and phenolic acids helps accelerate the recovery process of hemorrhoids, reducing the risk of infection and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes contain saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and chop them finely to ensure optimal potency and efficiency.
Combine the chopped leaves with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, pressing on the solids to extract all oils and essences.
Allow the resulting cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with hemorrhoids because they contain the soothing properties of yarrow flowers, which have been used for centuries to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, such as chamazulene and apigenin, work together to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and alleviating pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, yarrow's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help prevent infection and promote healing, providing long-lasting relief from hemorrhoidal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal yarrow creams containing flavonoids and sesquiterpenes help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in herbal yarrow creams help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms, promoting healing and protecting against tissue damage.

Vasoconstrictive property: The compounds found in herbal yarrow creams, such as thymol and terpinen-4-ol, help constrict blood vessels, which can reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of external or internal hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing irritated tissues and reducing swelling around the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems in the early morning or evening to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it thinly on paper towels or cloth for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium with 100 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup at 60°c to 70°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hemorrhoids are topical treatments that utilize plant extracts to alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids, such as itching, pain, and swelling. They work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the affected area. Some of the best herbal creams for hemorrhoids include Hamamelis virginiana, Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, Aesculus hippocastanum, Vaccinium myrtillus, Plantago lanceolata, Symphytum officinale, Aloe vera, Arnica montana, and Althaea officinalis. If left untreated, hemorrhoids can lead to painful bowel movements, discomfort during physical activity, and even anxiety or depression due to the constant discomfort and fear of bleeding.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hemorrhoids that are treated with herbal creams are anal fissures, itching and inflammation in the genital area, and perianal pruritus. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can provide relief from symptoms such as pain, discomfort, and itchiness. These natural remedies can help soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing inflammation and promoting healing without harsh chemicals or side effects.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hemorrhoids is a blend of Witch Hazel, Aloe Vera, and Tea Tree Oil. Witch Hazel reduces inflammation and soothes irritated skin, while Aloe Vera provides moisture and calms discomfort. Tea Tree Oil, with its antimicrobial properties, helps prevent infection and promotes healing. Applying these creams topically can help alleviate symptoms and promote faster recovery from hemorrhoids. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any new cream, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Pharmaceutical biology, herbal creams are beneficial for hemorrhoids due to the anti-inflammatory properties of the medicinal plants used to treat the condition. The study found that 26 medicinal plants, belonging to 19 families and 25 genera, were used by traditional healers in the Tabora region of Tanzania to manage hemorrhoids, with 12 of these plants reported for the first time for this purpose. By utilizing these plants, particularly those with high relative frequency citations such as Aloe vera, Allium sativum, and Psidium guajava, herbal creams can provide an effective and affordable treatment option for individuals suffering from hemorrhoids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant, also known as witch hazel, has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, reduce swelling and ease discomfort. By decreasing inflammation and promoting healing, Hamamelis virginiana creams provide relief from itching, pain, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids. This natural remedy can be applied topically to provide quick and effective relief.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Tannins: They act as astringents, constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling in the affected area, which helps to alleviate discomfort and promote healing.
Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent tissue damage, and promote the healing of hemorrhoidal tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hamamelis virginiana roots are used to make creams for hemorrhoids due to their high tannin content, which provides anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.

Barks: The barks of Hamamelis virginiana are used to make creams for hemorrhoids because they contain a high concentration of hamamelitannin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: Hamamelis virginiana leaves are used in creams for hemorrhoids due to their ability to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which helps to alleviate pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in late summer or early fall when they are at their highest potency.
Dry the hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in a single layer at 30-40°c for 2-3 days to preserve their properties.
Combine 1 part dried hamamelis virginiana with 2 parts vegetable oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally to maximize the extraction process.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort associated with the condition. The soothing gel extracted from the aloe vera plant calms irritated skin, providing relief from itching and pain. Its natural astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. By promoting healing and reducing inflammation, Aloe barbadensis creams can help alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids and provide quick relief.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These constituents help with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation, pain, and itching associated with hemorrhoidal swelling.
Polysaccharides: These constituents help with hemorrhoids by soothing and protecting the irritated and inflamed rectal mucosa, promoting healing and reducing bleeding.
Aloin: This constituent helps with hemorrhoids by acting as a natural laxative, promoting regular bowel movements and preventing constipation, which can exacerbate hemorrhoidal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe barbadensis leaves are primarily used to make creams for hemorrhoids due to their high concentration of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is often used, because of its high viscosity and its ability to provide a protective barrier on the skin.

Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: This is also used, as it contains various compounds such as vitamins, minerals and amino acids that help in reducing the inflammation and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 200g of aloe barbadensis gel with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160-180°f for 20-25 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Add 20g of candelilla wax and 10g of coconut oil to the mixture and heat for another 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes to an hour or until it reaches 100-120°f.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency similar to store-bought creams.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and saponins, reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected area. Calendula's antimicrobial properties also prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for tissue repair. Additionally, its emollient and moisturizing effects help to soften and protect the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster recovery from hemorrhoid symptoms. This natural remedy provides relief and promotes healing.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, thereby alleviating discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing pain and swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene): Act as an antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress and promoting wound healing, which may help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect irritated skin.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
Seeds: They are used due to their oil content, which helps to soften and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of calendula officinalis flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids immediately.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused liquid with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 120 degrees fahrenheit in about 20 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping into a creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood circulation. The active compounds in Aesculus hippocastanum, such as aescin, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, the cream's soothing and protective effects on the skin help to calm irritated areas and promote healing. By addressing the underlying causes of hemorrhoids, Aesculus hippocastanum creams provide relief from discomfort and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Aesculin: Aesculin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help soothe and protect the affected area from further irritation.
Saponins: Saponins possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm the discomfort and itching caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Barks: Aesculus hippocastanum barks are used to make creams for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: Aesculus hippocastanum seeds are used to make creams for hemorrhoids due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort.
Leaves: Aesculus hippocastanum leaves are used to make creams for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which help calm irritation and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100g of aesculus hippocastanum bark, 200g of petroleum jelly and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and petroleum jelly melt.
Add 20ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir well, then continue heating for another 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before stirring in 10g of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean container, allowing it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids. The tannins present in Vaccinium myrtillus also help to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the affected area and thus alleviating symptoms. Additionally, the cream's soothing and cooling effects can provide relief from discomfort and pain, making it an effective natural remedy for managing hemorrhoids. Its antioxidant properties also promote overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area, alleviating symptoms of hemorrhoids.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, pain, and itching associated with hemorrhoids, while its antioxidant properties protect against further damage.

Phenolic acids (specifically Gallic acid): Gallic acid's antimicrobial properties help prevent infections that can exacerbate hemorrhoids, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in tannins, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help soothe and shrink hemorrhoid tissues.
Fruits: The fruits of Vaccinium myrtillus are rich in anthocyanins and other antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in hemorrhoid tissues.

Leaves: The leaves of Vaccinium myrtillus also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help prevent infection and promote healing in hemorrhoid tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of vaccinium myrtillus berries in june, when they are ripe and have a deep blue color.
Dry the berries in a single layer at 25-30 degrees celsius for 48 hours to prevent mold growth.
Infuse 50g of the dried berries in 500ml of carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil, for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40-50 degrees celsius for 20 minutes, then pour into jars and cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata creams helps with hemorrhoids because they contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that reduce swelling and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing of damaged tissues in the anal region.
Apolipoprotein A-1: Help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected area, alleviating symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in astringent compounds, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: The roots of Plantago lanceolata contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm irritated skin around the anal area.
Barks: The bark of the plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in the healing of hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers, ideally in the morning, when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks, or until crispy.
Grind 20g of dried plantago lanceolata into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plantago lanceolata with 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 10g of shea butter, in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and then pour it into a tin container, allowing it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with hemorrhoids because they contain comfrey, a soothing and anti-inflammatory herb that reduces swelling and discomfort. The cream's active compounds, such as allantoin and flavonoids, have a calming effect on irritated tissues, promoting healing and reducing pain. By applying a topical cream rich in comfrey, individuals can alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids, including itching, bleeding, and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These help reduce inflammation and itching associated with hemorrhoids by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.

Glycosides: These help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the rectal area, promoting healing and reducing discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Flowers: The flowers of Symphytum officinale contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to promote healing and reduce swelling in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried roots in 100ml of carrier oil like jojoba oil at a temperature of 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate solids.
Add 30g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to the infused oil and melt the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and reaches a creamy consistency with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Fill the cooled and whipped mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal them tightly for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. The cream also provides a protective barrier on the skin, allowing it to heal faster and promoting the growth of new tissue. This natural remedy helps alleviate pain, itching, and inflammation, offering relief from the discomfort and irritation caused by hemorrhoids. Regular application can aid in faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate the discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Fatty acids: The fatty acids present in Aloe vera, particularly arachidonic and linoleic acids, possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm irritated tissues and reduce hemorrhoidal symptoms.

Glycoproteins: Glycoproteins in Aloe vera have been shown to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and improve tissue regeneration, all of which can contribute to the management and relief of hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracts from Aloe vera leaves are widely used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: This gel is used as a primary ingredient in creams due to its ability to reduce swelling and provide relief from hemorrhoid pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of aloe vera gel and store it in a bowl for further use.
Melt 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 20g of beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated.
Mix 10g of shea butter with the aloe vera gel and stir until smooth consistency.
Combine the aloe vera mixture with the melted coconut oil mixture and stir until well combined.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing pain. Additionally, Arnica montana creams have been found to enhance tissue repair, which can help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids, including itching, bleeding, and swelling. This natural remedy provides relief and support for individuals experiencing hemorrhoid discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help reduce inflammation and itching associated with hemorrhoids by constricting blood vessels and providing a soothing effect on the affected area.
Sesquiterpene lactones (arnic acid): These compounds help alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, making them beneficial for soothing and treating hemorrhoid symptoms.

Flavonoids (kaempferol): They help reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and provide antioxidant properties, which can contribute to the reduction of hemorrhoid discomfort and promotion of healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of arnica oil, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Arnica montana contain arnica oil and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them suitable for treating hemorrhoids.
Roots: The roots of Arnica montana are also used to create creams for hemorrhoids, as they contain arnica oil and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried arnica montana flowers in 250ml of cold-pressed carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container after 2 weeks.
Melt 50g of beeswax and 50g of cocoa butter in a double boiler at low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the strained oil with the melted beeswax and cocoa butter mixture in a ratio of 1:1.
Whisk the mixture until smooth and pour it into a clean glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The marshmallow root extract in the cream helps to calm and protect irritated tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, its natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and promote healing, which can alleviate pain and itching associated with hemorrhoids. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to keep the affected area hydrated and comfortable, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of further irritation.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: They help soothe and protect irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Glucans: They possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas, promoting healing and comfort.
Polysaccharides: They act as a protective barrier, preventing further irritation and discomfort, while also promoting wound healing and tissue repair.

Leaves: They also contain mucilage, which provides a protective barrier and helps reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: Similar to the roots, rhyzomes contain mucilage, making them a valuable ingredient in creams for hemorrhoids due to their soothing and protective properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 dried roots of althaea officinalis and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Combine the cleaned roots with 250ml of water in a saucepan and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Add 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to the strained liquid and heat gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.
